notes: Broad category encompassing CORBA and DCOM; succeeded by web services and microservices
CORBA became popular in mid-1990s then declined after early 2000s; still used in legacy telecom and defense systems
Microsoft dropped DCOM after failed attempts to scale; replaced by .NET Remoting then WCF then REST APIs
Core CORBA runtime component; niche use in legacy systems today
The Internet application platform the report identified as the key driver did indeed become dominant
